stories.title: macOS Tray Icon
stories.description:
Add a persistent macOS system tray (menu bar) icon for ToDoozy that provides quick access to the app and today's tasks.

**Tray Icon:**
- Monochrome template image (iconTemplate.png / iconTemplate@2x.png, white-on-transparent, 16x16 / 32x32) so macOS automatically adapts to light/dark menu bar. Generate this from the existing app icon (resources/icon.png) — create a simplified monochrome silhouette version.
- Badge shows the total count of non-done (open + in-progress) My Day tasks. No badge when count is 0.
- Tray icon is always visible while the app is running.

**Left-click menu:**
- 'Quick Add Task' at the top — triggers the same action as the quick-add keyboard shortcut (floating quick-add overlay, independent of main window).
- Up to 6 My Day tasks, split into two groups:
  - **In Progress** (top 3): sorted by the same ordering as the My Day view
  - **Open** (top 3): sorted by the same ordering as the My Day view
  - If either group has fewer than 3, the list is just shorter (the other group does NOT expand to fill).
- Each task shows: title + status indicator (the bucket/circle icon representing open vs in-progress). Done tasks excluded.
- If there are more non-done tasks beyond the 6 shown, show 'View all in My Day...' at the bottom which opens/focuses the main window on My Day.
- Clicking a task opens/focuses the main window and navigates to that task.

**Right-click context menu:**
- Open ToDoozy (shows/focuses main window)
- Add Task (triggers floating quick-add overlay)
- Quit ToDoozy (fully quits the app)

**Window close behavior:**
- Closing the window (red X) hides it to tray instead of quitting. The app continues running.
- Cmd+Q fully quits the app (standard macOS convention).
- Clicking dock icon re-opens the main window (app.on('activate')).

Before marking passes: true: read ui-reference.md and debug-learnings.md. Write tests for any new repository methods or utility functions. Run npm run test — all existing and new tests must pass. Run npm run typecheck — zero errors.
acceptance_criteria: Monochrome template icon appears in macOS menu bar on app launch
Badge displays count of non-done My Day tasks; updates in real-time as tasks change
Left-click shows menu with 'Quick Add Task' and up to 6 tasks (top 3 in-progress, then top 3 open), respecting My Day sort order
If more non-done tasks exist beyond the 6, 'View all in My Day...' appears at bottom
Clicking 'Quick Add Task' triggers floating quick-add overlay without showing main window
Clicking a task focuses main window and navigates to that task
Right-click context menu: Open, Add Task, Quit
Closing main window hides to tray; app stays running
Cmd+Q fully quits the app
Reopening from tray or dock icon restores the window

stories.title: Task Templates & Project Templates
stories.description:
Add a complete template system to ToDoozy with two template types: Task Templates and Project Templates.

**Task Templates:**
- Created via right-click context menu → 'Save as Template' on any task.
- Saves: title, description, priority, labels, recurrence, subtasks (full tree). Strips: due dates, snooze, completed dates. All statuses reset to default (Not Started).
- Templates are global — usable across any project.
- Templates view (Cmd+4) shows task templates in a flat, searchable list matching app styling.
- Actions on a task template: Use Template, Edit, Delete.
- **Use Template:** Shows a project picker dropdown (same style as My Day add-task project selector — color dot + project name). Creates the parent task + all subtasks in the target project. Labels that don't exist in the target project are auto-created. Opens the newly created task in the detail panel. Toast: 'Task created from template.'
- **Edit:** Opens the template in the detail panel. Same fields as a regular task but due date, snooze, completed date, and status changes are disabled/hidden. Edits save to the template, not a live task.

**Project Templates:**
- Created via a 'Save as Project Template' button/icon next to the project name in the project view header.
- Saves everything the project has: name, color, statuses, labels, tasks with full subtask tree, descriptions, priorities, recurrence. Strips: due dates, completed dates, snooze. All task statuses reset to default.
- Project templates appear in the Templates view under a 'Project Templates' section, below Task Templates. Flat, searchable list.
- Actions: View, Edit, Deploy, Delete.
- **Deploy:** Opens a multi-step wizard modal:
  - Step 1: Name & Color (pre-filled from template, editable)
  - Step 2: Statuses (pre-filled, add/remove/rename)
  - Step 3: Labels (pre-filled, add/remove/edit)
  - Step 4: Tasks (tree view of tasks/subtasks, add/remove/edit)
  - Step 5: Review & Create
  - 'Create Project' finalizes and creates the real project. 'Cancel' discards.
  - After creation, navigates to the new project.
- **Edit:** Same wizard flow but for modifying the template itself. Fields that don't apply (due dates, completion dates, snooze) are disabled.

**Templates View Structure:**
- Two sections: 'Task Templates' and 'Project Templates' with section headers (text-[10px] font-bold uppercase tracking-[0.3em]).
- Flat searchable list within each section.
- Search input at top filters both sections.
- Same styling as other views in the app.

Before marking passes: true: read ui-reference.md and debug-learnings.md. Write tests for any new repository methods or utility functions. Run npm run test — all existing and new tests must pass. Run npm run typecheck — zero errors.
acceptance_criteria: Right-click any task → 'Save as Template' creates a task template with title, description, priority, labels, recurrence, and subtasks; statuses reset, no due dates/snooze
Task templates are global (usable in any project)
Templates view shows Task Templates and Project Templates sections, both searchable
'Use Template' on a task template shows project picker, creates task + subtasks in target project, auto-creates missing labels, opens created task in detail panel
Editing a task template opens detail panel with due date, snooze, status, and completed date fields disabled
'Save as Project Template' button exists next to project name in project view header
Project template saves all project data (statuses, labels, tasks/subtasks, priorities, recurrence) with dates/snooze/completion stripped and statuses reset
'Deploy' opens a multi-step wizard (Name & Color → Statuses → Labels → Tasks → Review → Create Project)
Wizard fields are pre-filled from template and editable
'Create Project' in wizard creates the real project and navigates to it
'Cancel' in wizard discards without creating anything
Project templates can be viewed, edited, deployed, and deleted
Task templates can be used, edited, and deleted

stories.title: MCP Server for AI Integration
stories.description:
Add a Model Context Protocol (MCP) server to ToDoozy so AI assistants (Claude Code, Claude Desktop, etc.) can programmatically manage tasks, projects, labels, and more.

**Architecture:**
- Standalone Node.js script using @modelcontextprotocol/sdk (already a dependency), communicating via stdio transport.
- Talks directly to the same SQLite database the Electron app uses.
- Runs independently of Electron — AI client launches it as a subprocess.

**MCP Tools exposed:**
- **Tasks:** create, list, get, update, delete, complete, reopen, set_priority, set_due_date, set_description, set_recurrence, add_to_my_day, remove_from_my_day, snooze, archive, unarchive, duplicate, save_as_template
- **Subtasks:** create, list
- **Projects:** list, get, create, update, delete
- **Labels:** list, create, assign_to_task, remove_from_task
- **Statuses:** list (per project)
- **Search:** search tasks with filters (status, priority, label, due date, project, keyword)
- **My Day:** list today's tasks
- **Templates:** list, use_task_template, deploy_project_template

**Settings UI (Settings > MCP):**
- Toggle to enable/disable the MCP server
- 'Copy MCP Config' button — copies the JSON config snippet for pasting into Claude Desktop / Claude Code settings
- Shows the path to the MCP server script
- Status indicator showing if the server script is available

**Security:** stdio transport is inherently local (AI client spawns it as a subprocess), so no API key needed. The security boundary is the user's OS permissions.

Before marking passes: true: read ui-reference.md and debug-learnings.md. Write tests for any new repository methods or utility functions. Run npm run test — all existing and new tests must pass. Run npm run typecheck — zero errors.
acceptance_criteria: MCP server script exists and can be launched via stdio by an AI client
All task CRUD operations exposed as MCP tools (create, list, get, update, delete, complete, reopen)
Task metadata tools work: set_priority, set_due_date, set_description, set_recurrence, snooze, archive/unarchive, duplicate, save_as_template, add/remove from My Day
Subtask tools: create and list subtasks under a parent
Project tools: list, get, create, update, delete
Label tools: list, create, assign_to_task, remove_from_task
Status tools: list statuses per project
Search tool filters by status, priority, label, due date, project, and keyword
My Day tool lists today's tasks
Template tools: list templates, use task template, deploy project template
Settings > MCP section with enable/disable toggle
'Copy MCP Config' button copies valid JSON config for Claude Desktop / Claude Code
Server path is displayed in settings
MCP server reads from the same SQLite database as the Electron app

stories.title: Rich Text Editor with Tiptap
stories.description:
Replace the current markdown textarea + preview split in DetailDescription.tsx with a Tiptap WYSIWYG editor that stores markdown in the database. No data migration needed — existing markdown descriptions render correctly in Tiptap.

**Dependencies:**
- @tiptap/react, @tiptap/starter-kit, @tiptap/extension-link, @tiptap/extension-image, @tiptap/extension-task-list, @tiptap/extension-task-item, @tiptap/extension-placeholder, tiptap-markdown

**Editor:**
- Replaces the edit/preview toggle with a single WYSIWYG surface
- Stores markdown in the DB via tiptap-markdown (serialize on save, parse on load)
- 1-second autosave debounce (matches existing behavior)
- Placeholder: 'Add a description...'
- Escape blurs the editor

**Toolbar — Fixed (above editor):**
- Block-level controls: Heading 1/2/3 dropdown, Bullet List, Numbered List, Checklist, Code Block, Horizontal Rule, Image, Attach File

**Toolbar — Floating bubble (on text selection):**
- Inline controls: Bold, Italic, Strikethrough, Inline Code, Link

**Link editing:**
- Cmd+K when editor is focused inserts/edits a link (does NOT open command palette)
- Cmd+K when editor is NOT focused opens command palette as usual
- Clicking an existing link shows a popover with: display text, URL, Edit button, Remove button
- Auto-link detection: pasting a URL auto-converts to a clickable link

**Slash commands:**
- Type / to open a menu with: Heading 1, Heading 2, Heading 3, Bullet List, Numbered List, Checklist, Code Block, Image, Attach File, Horizontal Rule
- Arrow keys navigate, Enter selects, Escape dismisses

**Image support:**
- Cmd+V paste images as base64 (same as current, 10MB limit)
- Image insert via slash command or toolbar button
- Images render inline in the editor

**Attach File:**
- Button in toolbar and slash command menu
- Greyed out with tooltip 'Configure cloud storage in Settings to attach files' until cloud storage is configured
- Cloud storage configuration is a separate future story

**Code blocks:**
- Monospace font, no syntax highlighting
- Enter inside code block continues the block, Cmd+Enter exits

**Checklist:**
- Interactive checkboxes (clickable in the editor)
- Stored as - [ ] / - [x] in markdown

**Keyboard shortcuts:**
- Cmd+B: Bold, Cmd+I: Italic, Cmd+Shift+S: Strikethrough, Cmd+E: Inline code
- Cmd+K: Insert/edit link (when editor focused)
- Cmd+Shift+7: Numbered list, Cmd+Shift+8: Bullet list, Cmd+Shift+9: Checklist

Before marking passes: true: read ui-reference.md and debug-learnings.md. Write tests for any new repository methods or utility functions. Run npm run test — all existing and new tests must pass. Run npm run typecheck — zero errors.
acceptance_criteria: Tiptap WYSIWYG editor replaces the textarea + preview toggle in DetailDescription
Existing markdown descriptions render correctly without migration
Description is stored as markdown in the database
Fixed toolbar shows block-level controls (headings, lists, checklist, code block, HR, image, attach file)
Floating bubble toolbar appears on text selection with inline controls (bold, italic, strikethrough, code, link)
Slash command menu opens on / with all block types
Cmd+K inserts/edits link when editor is focused, opens command palette otherwise
Clicking a link shows edit popover with display text, URL, Edit, and Remove
Pasting URLs auto-converts to clickable links
Image paste works (base64, 10MB limit)
Attach File button is greyed out with tooltip when cloud storage is not configured
Checklist items are interactive (clickable checkboxes)
Code blocks render in monospace without syntax highlighting
1-second autosave debounce
All standard keyboard shortcuts work (Cmd+B, Cmd+I, etc.)
Escape blurs the editor

stories.title: Global App Toggle Shortcut
stories.description:
Add a global keyboard shortcut (default Cmd+Shift+B) that toggles the main ToDoozy window from anywhere in macOS. Mirrors the existing quick-add shortcut pattern exactly.

**Behavior:**
- App running but not focused → bring main window to front (unminimize if needed, switch to its Space/desktop)
- App focused → minimize to system tray
- App not running → launch it (only works when installed, not testable in dev)

**Implementation (mirror quick-add shortcut pattern):**
- `src/shared/shortcut-utils.ts` — add `DEFAULT_APP_TOGGLE_SHORTCUT = 'CommandOrControl+Shift+B'`
- `src/main/index.ts` — add `registerAppToggleShortcut()` and `loadAndRegisterAppToggleShortcut()` alongside existing quick-add equivalents. Callback checks `hasAuthSession()`, then: if main window is focused → hide to tray; otherwise → show, restore, focus, move to active Space.
- `src/main/ipc-handlers.ts` — add `app-toggle:updateShortcut` handler (same validation as `quickadd:updateShortcut`)
- `src/preload/index.ts` + `index.d.ts` — expose `appToggle.updateShortcut(accelerator)` on the bridge
- Settings > General — add a second `ShortcutRecorder` for 'Show/Hide App' using setting key `app_toggle_shortcut`
- Persist via SettingsRepository with key `app_toggle_shortcut`
- Unregister on `will-quit` (already covered by `globalShortcut.unregisterAll()`)

Before marking passes: true: read ui-reference.md and debug-learnings.md. Write tests for any new repository methods or utility functions. Run npm run test — all existing and new tests must pass. Run npm run typecheck — zero errors.
acceptance_criteria: Pressing Cmd+Shift+B (default) when app is not focused brings the main window to the front
Pressing Cmd+Shift+B when app is focused minimizes it to the system tray
If the main window is minimized, the shortcut restores and focuses it
If the main window is on another desktop/Space, the shortcut switches to that Space
Works when the app is in the system tray only (no visible window)
The shortcut is configurable in Settings > General via a ShortcutRecorder component
Reserved macOS shortcuts are rejected (same validation as quick-add)
The configured shortcut persists across app restarts
Changing the shortcut immediately unregisters the old one and registers the new one
The shortcut only works when the user has an active auth session

stories.title: Pomodoro Timer
stories.description:
Add a per-task Pomodoro timer system that minimizes the app to tray and shows a live countdown in the macOS menu bar.

**Timer Presets:**
- Default presets: 10 min, 25 min, 50 min
- Custom presets configurable in Settings > Timer (add/remove with name + duration)
- One timer can run at a time — starting a new one stops the current

**Starting a timer:**
- **Play button** appears on hover after the last character of the task title (or after truncation point) in all views (My Day, Projects, tray menu). Subtle monochrome icon consistent with existing hover action patterns. Left-click opens a popup.
- **Popup:** Uses existing popup patterns (portal to document.body, z-[9999], surface bg, border, shadow, motion-safe:animate-in fade-in zoom-in duration-100, positioned via getBoundingClientRect). If repetition mode is enabled in settings, shows the default repetition count with the number selected/editable for quick override. Enter confirms. If repetition mode is off, starts the default timer immediately.
- **Context menu:** Right-click a task shows a 'Start Timer' flyout submenu listing all configured timer presets. Not shown when multiple tasks are selected.
- **Tray menu:** Play button appears on hover over tasks in the tray left-click menu, same behavior.
- Starting a timer auto-moves the task to the first in-progress status (via findProjectStatusForBucket) if it was in a 'not started' state.

**Active timer — tray behavior:**
- App minimizes to tray when timer starts.
- Menu bar shows countdown text next to the tray icon (e.g., '18:32').
- If in repetition mode, shows a small indicator like '3/5' alongside the countdown.
- Tray menu during active timer shows ONLY the countdown and controls — no My Day tasks, no quick add.
- Hovering over the countdown in the tray menu reveals Pause/Resume and Stop controls.

**Timer completion:**
- Sound notification (configurable on/off in settings).
- System notification (configurable on/off in settings). Clicking the system notification opens the app and navigates to that task.
- Break timer auto-starts after work timer finishes. Default 5 min, configurable in settings.
- After break finishes: sound notification only (no system notification), tray returns to normal.
- Break auto-start behavior configurable in settings.

**Repetition/session mode:**
- Configurable in Settings: on/off toggle, default repetition count.
- When enabled, runs work-break cycles for N repetitions (e.g., 5x 20min work + 5min break).
- After all repetitions complete, tray returns to normal state.
- Perpetual mode option: runs indefinitely until manually stopped.
- Stop-after-X-repetitions option.

**Activity log:**
- Logs 'Completed 25 min focus session' with timestamp on the task's activity log after each work timer completes.

**Timer state:**
- Timer is lost if the app is quit mid-timer (no persistence across restarts).

**Settings > Timer:**
- Default timer duration (dropdown of configured presets)
- Custom timer presets (add/remove, each with a name and duration in minutes)
- Default break duration (default: 5 min)
- Repetition mode on/off
- Default repetition count
- Perpetual mode on/off
- Sound notification on/off
- System notification on/off

**Visual consistency:**
- All UI elements (play button, popup, tray controls, settings section) follow existing app patterns: typography scale, monochrome-first design, hover/selection styles, shared components. Play button matches existing hover action patterns. Popup uses same portal/animation/styling as InputSuggestionPopup, LabelPicker, etc. Settings section matches existing settings tab patterns.

Before marking passes: true: read ui-reference.md and debug-learnings.md. Write tests for any new repository methods or utility functions. Run npm run test — all existing and new tests must pass. Run npm run typecheck — zero errors.
acceptance_criteria: Play button appears on hover after task title in all views (My Day, Projects, tray menu)
Left-clicking play opens popup; if repetition mode on, shows editable rep count; if off, starts timer immediately
Right-click context menu has 'Start Timer' flyout with all configured presets (not shown for multi-select)
Starting a timer minimizes app to tray and shows countdown text in menu bar
Countdown shows 'MM:SS' format with optional 'N/N' repetition indicator
Hovering tray countdown reveals Pause/Resume and Stop controls
Tray menu during timer shows only countdown and controls
Timer completion plays sound notification (if enabled in settings)
Timer completion shows system notification (if enabled); clicking it opens the task
Break timer auto-starts after work timer; duration configurable in settings
Repetition mode cycles work-break for N reps then stops
Perpetual mode runs until manually stopped
Starting a timer auto-moves task to first in-progress status if not started
Activity log records completed focus sessions with duration and timestamp
Timer state is not persisted across app restarts
Settings > Timer section with all configuration options (presets, break duration, repetition, notifications)
Only one timer can run at a time
Default presets: 10 min, 25 min, 50 min; custom presets addable in settings
All visuals follow existing app patterns (typography, colors, shared components, popup styling)
